member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Code128Encoder
member.summary: Pure, reflection-free Code 128 encoder (Code Set B — all printable ASCII 32–126). Produces the module pattern (true = bar) of the full symbol: Start B · data · weighted modulo-103 check symbol · Stop. The width table is the published Code 128 table, so output can be checked against reference symbols; an independent round-trips the pattern back to text.

member.name: F:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Code128Encoder.MinChar
member.summary: The lowest / highest ASCII codes Code Set B represents.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Code128Encoder.SymbolValues(System.String)
member.summary: The symbol values for the text, including the leading Start B but excluding the check symbol and Stop. Each printable ASCII char maps to value (char − 32).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Code128Encoder.Checksum(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Int32})
member.summary: The weighted modulo-103 check symbol: the start value (weight 1) plus each data value times its 1-based position, mod 103. Matches the published worked example (PJJ123C with Start A → 54).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Code128Encoder.Encode(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes text into the full Code 128B module pattern (true = bar).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Code128Encoder.Decode(System.Boolean[])
member.summary: Independent decoder: reads a Code 128B module pattern back to its text, verifying the check symbol. Used to round-trip-verify .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Ean13Encoder
member.summary:
Pure, reflection-free EAN-13 encoder. Turns a 12- or 13-digit string into the 95-module bar pattern of the symbol (true = dark bar). The tables and structure follow the EAN-13 / UPC standard exactly, so the output can be checked against published reference symbols.
Structure: start guard 101 · six left digits (7 modules each, encoded with L/G parity chosen by the first digit) · centre guard 01010 · six right digits (7 modules each, R parity) · end guard 101 — 95 modules.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Ean13Encoder.CheckDigit(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Computes the EAN-13 check digit for the first 12 digits (positions weighted 1, 3, 1, 3, …). Returns a value 0–9.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Ean13Encoder.Normalize(System.String)
member.summary: Normalises input to a full 13-digit code: validates digits, appends the check digit when given 12, and verifies it when given 13.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Ean13Encoder.Encode(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a 12- or 13-digit code into its 95-module bar pattern (true = bar).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.GaloisField
member.summary: Arithmetic over GF(256) with the QR-code primitive polynomial 0x11D and a generator of 2. Precomputes exponent/log tables so multiply is a table lookup. Pure and allocation-free at call time.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.GaloisField.Exponent(System.Int32)
member.summary: The field element α^.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.GaloisField.Multiply(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiplies two field elements.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrCode
member.summary: Pure, reflection-free QR encoder (versions 1–4; numeric, alphanumeric, and byte modes; all four error-correction levels). Heavy lifting — Galois-field Reed-Solomon, bitstream assembly, module placement, masking — lives in the Qr internals; this is the public entry point.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrCode.Encode(System.String,BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrErrorLevel,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Encodes into a QR symbol at the given error-correction level, picking the most compact mode and smallest fitting version (1–4).
member.param: Forces a specific data mask (0–7) instead of penalty-based selection. Intended for verification against fixed reference symbols; leave null in normal use.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrEncoder
member.summary: Builds the QR data codeword stream: picks a mode and version, encodes the payload bitstream with terminator and padding, splits it into Reed-Solomon blocks, and interleaves data and EC codewords per the standard. Pure and reflection-free. Supports versions 1–4.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrEncoder.SelectMode(System.String)
member.summary: Picks the most compact mode that can represent the text.

member.summary: The number of payload data bits the text needs in the given mode.

member.summary: The smallest supported version that fits the payload at the given level.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrEncoder.BuildDataCodewords(System.String,BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrMode,System.Int32,BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrErrorLevel)
member.summary: Encodes the text into the full set of data codewords (concatenated across all blocks), including mode, character count, terminator, and pad codewords.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrEncoder.InterleaveWithEc(System.Int32[],System.Int32,BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrErrorLevel)
member.summary: Splits data codewords into blocks, computes EC codewords for each, then interleaves data then EC column-by-column per the standard, producing the final codeword stream laid into the matrix.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrEncoder.BitWriter
member.summary: A minimal MSB-first bit accumulator used to build the codeword stream.

member.summary: QR data-mask conditions and the four penalty rules used to choose the mask that scans most reliably. Pure and reflection-free.

member.summary: Whether mask pattern (0–7) flips module (row, col).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrMask.Penalty(System.Boolean[0:,0:])
member.summary: The total penalty score of a finished module grid (lower is better).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Barcodes.Qr.QrMatrix
member.summary: Lays the QR module matrix: function patterns (finders, separators, timing, alignment, dark module), the zig-zag data placement, mask selection by penalty, and the BCH-coded format information. Pure and reflection-free. Versions 1–4.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive
member.summary: Tier 1 headless file manager. Maintains the expanded/selected folder state, flattens the folder hierarchy for the navigation pane, exposes the selected folder's contents and its breadcrumb path, and routes "open" actions (drill into a folder, or raise a file-open event). Also owns a mutable working copy of the tree so items can be moved between folders (drag-and-drop, or the keyboard/single-pointer alternative) without mutating the immutable records supplied via . Renders nothing itself.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive.OnFileOpen
member.summary: Raised when a file (not a folder) is opened.

member.summary: Raised when the selected folder changes.

member.summary: Raised after an item is moved into a folder (or to the root when null).

member.summary: Human-readable result of the last move/upload/delete, for an aria-live region.

member.summary: Selects a folder, expanding its ancestors so it is visible in the tree.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive.OpenEntryAsync(BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileSystemEntry)
member.summary: Opens an entry: drills into folders, raises for files.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive.ToggleMoveCandidateAsync(BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileSystemEntry)
member.summary: Moves into (or to the root when is null). This is the single move primitive shared by native drag-and-drop and the keyboard/single-pointer "move" buttons (the WCAG 2.5.7 alternative). A move is rejected — and announced as such — when the target is the item itself, the item's current parent (a no-op), or a descendant of the item (which would orphan the subtree).

Marks (or unmarks) an item as the one to move via the keyboard/single-pointer path: the first press arms the move, then activating a folder's "Move here" control places it. A second press on the same item cancels. This is the WCAG 2.5.7 "mark then place" alternative to the drag gesture — no pointer drag, no JS.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive.PlaceMoveCandidateAsync(BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileSystemEntry)
member.summary: Places the currently-marked item into (root when null).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive.FocusAfterMoveAsync(BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileSystemEntry)
member.summary: Hook for the styled tier to move keyboard focus after a successful move. The headless primitive renders nothing, so this is a no-op here; the DOM-aware subclass overrides it.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileManagerPrimitive.MoveOutcome
member.summary: Why a move was (or was not) performed.

member.summary: Records an upload result for the aria-live region (4.1.3).

member.summary: Hook for the styled tier to move keyboard focus to the status region after an upload (the uploaded files are not yet visible rows — streaming is the host's job), so keyboard/SR users land on the announcement (WCAG 2.4.3). No-op here.

member.summary: A callable AI tool: a name, a description, a JSON-Schema for its arguments, and an async invocation. exposes a set of these over the Model Context Protocol so an assistant can call them.

member.summary: The JSON-Schema object describing the tool's arguments.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IAiTool.IsReadOnly
member.summary: Whether the tool only reads (safe to auto-run) vs. mutates state (treat with care).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IAiTool.InvokeAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the tool with a JSON object of arguments. The token cancels in-flight work.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IAiToolAuthorizer
member.summary: Decides whether the current caller may see and invoke a given . A host implements this over its own auth (e.g. an ASP.NET Core ClaimsPrincipal + authorization policies). consults it for every tools/list and tools/call, so a tool the caller may not use is never advertised and never runs. When no authorizer is supplied, all tools are allowed.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.FormAiTool`1
member.summary: Turns a into a callable : an AI call's arguments are applied to a fresh model and validated with the form's own rules; if valid, runs and its text is returned, otherwise the validation errors are returned as an error result so the model can correct itself.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.FormAiTool`1.IsReadOnly
member.summary: A form tool writes by default; pass isReadOnly: true for query-only forms.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.DxFormModelAttribute
member.summary: Marks a class as a BlazorDX form model. The BlazorDX.SourceGen generator emits a strongly-typed for it — field metadata, validation, and zero-reflection get/set — which both DxForm renders and projects into an AI tool (JSON-Schema) definition. One model, two faces: a UI a person fills, and a tool an AI calls.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.DxFormModelAttribute.Name
member.summary: Tool name exposed to an AI host (defaults to the type name). Use snake_case for function-calling hosts.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.DxFormModelAttribute.Description
member.summary: What the form/tool does — shown to the AI so it knows when to call it.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.DxFieldAttribute
member.summary: Declares a property as a form field. The label, description, and constraints feed both the rendered input (and its validation) and the generated AI tool schema — the in particular is what an AI reads to fill the field.

member.summary: Hide this field from the AI tool surface: it is omitted from the generated JSON-Schema and refuses to set it from AI arguments — yet a human still sees and edits it in DxForm. Use for PII / secrets the model should neither see nor write. Equivalent to applying .

member.summary: Marks a form-field property as off-limits to AI: excluded from the generated tool schema and never settable via , while remaining fully editable by a human in DxForm. The same effect as [DxField(Sensitive = true)], but it composes with plain DataAnnotations-annotated models too.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.FormTool
member.summary: Projects a into an AI tool and back: it emits a JSON-Schema tool definition an AI host can call (the shape shared by the Model Context Protocol and OpenAI/Anthropic function-calling), and applies a tool call's JSON arguments onto a model instance — then validates with the very same rules the rendered form uses. So a form a person fills and a tool an AI invokes are one thing. All JSON is built by hand / parsed with — no reflection, AOT- and trim-safe.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.FormTool.BuildInputSchema``1(BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IFormModel{``0})
member.summary: Builds the JSON-Schema object describing the model's parameters (types, constraints, descriptions, and the required set).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.FormTool.BuildToolDefinition``1(BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IFormModel{``0})
member.summary: Builds the full tool definition envelope { name, description, input_schema } (MCP / Anthropic shape; OpenAI nests the same schema under parameters).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.FormTool.ApplyArguments``1(BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IFormModel{``0},``0,System.String)
member.summary: Applies an AI tool call's JSON arguments to (only the fields present are set), then returns the validation result. Invalid JSON yields a single error rather than throwing.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpStdioHost
member.summary: Runs an over a stdio transport: newline-delimited JSON-RPC read from and written to . This is how local assistants (e.g. Claude Desktop) connect — they spawn a process and speak MCP over its stdin/stdout. Per JSON-RPC, notifications (requests with no id) get no reply. Reflection-free and dependency-free, so the same host runs on any .NET target.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpStdioHost.RunAsync(BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer,System.IO.TextReader,System.IO.TextWriter,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Pumps requests from through until the input ends or is cancelled. Each non-empty line is one JSON-RPC message; each request's response is written as one line.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer
member.summary: A minimal, transport-agnostic Model Context Protocol tool server. Register s (e.g. built from a [DxFormModel]) and feed it JSON-RPC requests; it answers initialize, tools/list, and tools/call. Wire to any transport (stdio, a SSE/HTTP endpoint) to expose a BlazorDX form to an AI assistant. Pure JSON (built by hand / parsed with ) — no reflection.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer.ServerName
member.summary: Server name advertised during initialize.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer.Authorizer
member.summary: Optional authorization gate. When set, a tool the caller may not use is neither listed nor callable (an unauthorized tools/call looks exactly like an unknown tool, so the server never reveals that a privileged tool exists). When null, all tools are allowed.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer.Diagnostics
member.summary: Optional audit/observability sink. Every tools/call — allowed, denied, ok, or errored — is reported here, giving an audit trail of what the AI did. When null, no-op.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer.Add(BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.IAiTool)
member.summary: Registers a tool (replacing any with the same name). Fluent.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer.Tools
member.summary: The registered tools, by name.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Forms.McpToolServer.HandleAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles one JSON-RPC 2.0 request and returns the JSON-RPC response string.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.DataGridPrimitive`1
member.summary: Footer and per-group aggregates for . Kept in its own partial so the core grid file stays under the 1000-line cap (this is the aggregation concern). Aggregates are computed over the in-memory Items via the compute backend; they are not available in remote mode.
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.
member.summary:
Tier 1 headless data grid: owns row ordering, grouping, the virtualization window, sorting, aggregates, and ARIA wiring, but renders nothing on its own. A Tier 2 component (or a custom subclass) supplies the markup by overriding and reading the protected state below.
Rendering is driven by a flat list of (group headers interleaved with data rows), so one windowing implementation serves both the grouped and ungrouped views.
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.
member.summary: CSV export for . Kept in its own partial so the core grid file stays under the 1000-line cap (this is the export concern, not the data/ordering concern).
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.
member.summary: In-memory row grouping for : buckets the current (filtered, sorted) row order by the grouped column into collapsible groups, and flattens them — a header per group plus the rows of expanded groups — into the slot list that drives virtualization. Server-side grouping lives in the RemoteGroup partial; this is the path used when all rows are held in memory.

Keyboard cell navigation for (the ARIA "grid" pattern). Kept in its own partial so the core grid file stays under the 1000-line cap — this is the navigation concern, not the data concern.
The active cell is addressed by a slot position (into the full, virtualized slot list) plus a display-column position. Focus stays on the grid body and the active cell is pointed to with aria-activedescendant, so navigation works regardless of which rows are currently realized in the DOM. When the active cell crosses the rendered window the grid scrolls it back into view.
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.
member.summary: PDF export for . Sits beside the CSV and Excel exports so the core grid file stays under the 1000-line cap; the actual PDF generation lives in .
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.
member.summary: Server-side data binding for . Kept in its own partial: when is set the grid enters , where it no longer holds all rows. It fetches only the virtualization window, caches loaded rows by absolute index, and pushes sort/filter down to the source. Grouping, aggregates, in-memory selection, and inline edit are in-memory features and are disabled in remote mode.

Server-side grouping for . When the bound implements and a group column is chosen, the grid asks the server for group summaries (key + count + aggregates) up front and renders collapsed headers, then lazily fetches each group's rows — scoped by the group key — only when it is expanded. So grouping and subtotals scale past memory. Kept in its own partial to hold the core grid file under the cap.

Layout persistence for : capture the user's column order / widths / hidden columns / sort / filters as a serializable , and restore it later. fires on every layout mutation so a host can auto-save (e.g. to localStorage or a profile).
Excel (.xlsx) export for . Sits in its own partial alongside the CSV export so the core grid file stays under the 1000-line cap; the actual OOXML packaging lives in .
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.GridGroupSummary
member.summary: One group as summarized by the server: the group key (the grouped column's text), how many rows it holds, and any requested per-group aggregates. member.summary: The set of groups for a grouped column, as summarized by a data source.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.IGridGroupDataSource`1
member.summary: A server-side data source that can also group. When a grid is bound to one of these and a group column is chosen, the grid asks the server for group summaries (key + count + aggregates) up front, then lazily fetches each group's rows through the inherited — with the group's key added as a filter on the grouped column — only when that group is expanded. So grouping and subtotals scale to data far larger than memory.
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through the generated accessor.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.PdfWriter
member.summary: A tiny, dependency-free writer for a tabular PDF document. It emits a valid PDF 1.4 file by hand — catalog, page tree, the two built-in Helvetica fonts (so nothing has to be embedded), and one uncompressed content stream per page — with a byte-accurate cross-reference table. No third-party library, no reflection: AOT- and trim-safe, so it runs unchanged in the browser runtime.
member.remarks: Rows are laid out as a simple grid on US-Letter pages, the header repeated in bold at the top of each page; cell text is left-aligned and truncated to its column width. Text uses WinAnsi (ASCII) — characters outside it become '?'.

member.name: F:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.PdfWriter.MimeType
member.summary: The IANA media type browsers expect for a .pdf download.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.PdfWriter.Write(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String}})
member.summary: Builds a paginated table PDF: form a bold, per-page header row, then each item of is a row.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.PivotGridPrimitive`1
member.summary: Tier 1 headless pivot table (cross-tab). Buckets rows by a row field and a column field, then aggregates a numeric value field within each bucket using the compute backend (Rust in the browser, managed C# on the server) — the same kernels the data grid uses. Row, column, and grand totals are aggregated from the underlying values (not from the cells), so means stay correct. Renders nothing itself; a Tier 2 component supplies the table markup.
member.typeparam: The row type, bound through a generated accessor.

member.summary: Column index whose distinct values become pivot columns.

member.summary: Numeric column index aggregated into the cells.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.XlsxWriter
member.summary: A tiny, dependency-free writer for the Office Open XML spreadsheet (.xlsx) format. It assembles the minimal set of OOXML parts (content types, package relationships, a workbook, a styles part, and one worksheet) into a ZIP package by hand — no third-party library, no reflection, AOT- and trim-safe, so it runs unchanged in the browser WebAssembly runtime.
member.remarks: Cells are written as inline strings, except values that round-trip exactly as an invariant number (so "10" becomes a real numeric cell while "007" stays text). The first row is rendered bold via a single styled cell format.

member.name: F:BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.XlsxWriter.MimeType
member.summary: The IANA media type browsers expect for an .xlsx download.

member.summary: Builds a single-worksheet workbook: become a bold first row, then each item of becomes a row.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Inputs.DatePickerPrimitive
member.summary: Tier 1 headless date picker: a trigger that opens an anchored calendar with month navigation, day selection, and 2D keyboard navigation. Focus stays on the calendar grid; the focused day is surfaced via aria-activedescendant (the WAI-ARIA grid pattern). Composes the anchored-positioning and dismissal primitives; renders no markup itself.

member.summary: Tier 1 headless tile dashboard: a set of tiles the user can reorder by dragging a tile's header onto another, or with the keyboard (Alt+Arrow moves the focused tile; Arrow alone moves focus). Order is a permutation over the tile list — the tiles themselves never move — so it composes with the shared reorder helper. Native HTML5 drag, no JS interop. Renders no markup itself.

member.summary: Single-pointer (no-drag) reorder: moves the tile at by positions. This is the WCAG 2.5.7 alternative to the drag gesture, for pointer users who cannot press-hold-and-drag. Clamped by , so a no-op at the ends.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Motion.PresenceBoundary
member.summary: Delays a child's removal from the DOM so it can animate out — the piece Blazor lacks natively (an @if that turns false destroys the node at once). When flips to false, the boundary keeps the child mounted, swaps in to trigger the CSS exit transition, waits , then releases it. This is the AnimatePresence equivalent described in ADR 0005.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Motion.PresenceBoundary.EnterClass
member.summary: CSS class applied while the child is entering/visible.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Motion.PresenceBoundary.LeaveClass
member.summary: CSS class applied while the child is animating out.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Motion.PresenceBoundary.ExitDurationMs
member.summary: How long to keep the child mounted after it is hidden, in milliseconds.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Navigation.RovingTabIndex
member.summary: Roving-tabindex state: tracks which item in a list is the active (tabbable) one and moves it with arrow / Home / End semantics, wrapping around and skipping disabled items. This is the keyboard-navigation backbone shared by Menu, Listbox, Select, ComboBox, RadioGroup, and grid cells. Pure logic — no Blazor or DOM dependency — so it is unit-testable in isolation.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Navigation.RovingTabIndex.ActiveIndex
member.summary: The active index, or -1 when nothing is active.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Navigation.RovingTabIndex.Configure(System.Int32,System.Func{System.Int32,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Sets the item count and an optional enabled predicate (to skip disabled items).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Navigation.RovingTabIndex.MoveTo(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the active item directly (ignored if out of range or disabled).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Navigation.TabItem
member.summary: One tab: a title and the content shown when it is selected.

member.summary: One tab: a title and the content shown when it is selected.

member.summary: Roving state shared with the styled layer for focus/tabindex.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Overlays.ComboBoxPrimitive`1
member.summary: Tier 1 headless combo box: a text input that filters options (typeahead) with an anchored dropdown and single selection. Focus stays in the input; the active option is tracked via roving state and surfaced through aria-activedescendant, the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern. Composes positioning, dismissal, and roving-tabindex. Renders no markup itself.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Overlays.PopoverPrimitive
member.summary: Tier 1 headless popover: a floating panel anchored to a trigger. member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Scheduling.SchedulerEvent
member.summary: A scheduled event placed on the calendar.
member.param: Event label.
Start timestamp.
End timestamp. May fall on a later day than ; the event then renders on every day in its [Start..End] date range (a multi-day month span or a midnight-crossing block on each touched day column).
Optional CSS color override for the event block.
Optional category/status label. Conveyed as text (never color alone) so the distinction survives for colour-blind and high-contrast users (WCAG 1.4.1).
member.remarks: DEFERRED: RRULE-style recurrence is not yet modelled — every event is a single concrete interval. Drag-to-move / drag-to-create editing is not implemented, and the overlap-lane layout is computed in pure C# (the planned Rust overlap-lane kernel is not wired up).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Scheduling.SchedulerPrimitive
member.summary: Tier 1 headless scheduler. Computes the visible cells for the Week, Month, and Day views and lays each event out (time-positioned blocks for Week/Day, day buckets for Month). Owns view selection, navigation, and a 2-D roving active-cell model (the ARIA "grid" keyboard pattern, mirroring the DataGrid); renders nothing — the styled layer turns the model into markup.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Scheduling.SchedulerPrimitive.Blocks
member.summary: Lays every event into one block per visible day it touches (Week/Day). A multi-day or midnight-crossing event yields a separate block on each day column it overlaps, with start/end clamped to that day's visible hour range.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Scheduling.SchedulerPrimitive.EventsOn(System.DateOnly)
member.summary: Events that touch — i.e. whose [Start..End] date range includes it — ordered by start time (Month cells). A multi-day event therefore appears in every spanned cell.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Scheduling.SchedulerPrimitive.MoveActiveCell(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Applies an arrow / Home / End / PageUp / PageDown key to the active cell. Returns true when the key was a navigation key (so the caller can re-render and prevent default). Cells clamp at the grid edges (no wrap).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Primitives.Selection.SelectionState`1
member.summary: Tracks selected values for single- or multiple-selection components (Select, ComboBox, Listbox, DataGrid rows, TransferList). Pure logic — no Blazor or DOM dependency — so it is unit-testable in isolation. Values are compared with the default equality comparer for .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Selection.SelectionState`1.Multiple
member.summary: When true, multiple values may be selected; otherwise selecting replaces.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Selection.SelectionState`1.Selected
member.summary: The selected values, in no particular order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Primitives.Selection.SelectionState`1.Current
member.summary: The single selected value (or default when nothing is selected).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Selection.SelectionState`1.Set(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Replaces the selection with the given values.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Primitives.Selection.SelectionState`1.Toggle(`0)
member.summary: Toggles a value. In single mode this selects exactly the value; in multiple mode it adds the value if absent or removes it if present.
